Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-08-04 section=business period_end=2023-06-30
Item 1. Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is focused on providing branded products of superior quality and value to improve the lives of the world's consumers, now and for generations to come. The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements. Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries. Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC. The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov. Reports can also be accessed through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com. P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference and the information on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report.
Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
. Our business model is built to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth and value creation. We rely on 
…[truncated 3692 chars]…
ten holding a leadership or significant market share position. We support our products with advertising, promotions and other marketing vehicles to build awareness and trial of our brands and products in conjunction with our sales force. We believe this combination provides the
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
most efficient method of marketing for these types of products. Product quality, performance, value and packaging are also important differentiating factors.
Government Regulation
. Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and data privacy and protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2024 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent including diversity, equality and inclusion as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2023, the Company had approximately 107,000 employees, an increase of 1% versus the prior year due primarily to business growth. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 27% of our employees are located in the United States. 41% of our global employees are women and 30% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining skilled and diverse talent, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from among the best universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide. We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers. We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels. Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance in line with local country practices with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy to offer consumers irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. Our aim is to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth, value creation and key sustainability objectives. In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition and published a Climate Transition Action
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Plan, which describes the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. This includes a long-term objective of net zero emissions for scopes 1 and 2, elements of scope 3 and interim goals to help us pace our progress. The Company has also declared goals towards using renewable electricity for our operations, reducing use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, increasing the recyclability or reusability of packaging, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects to address water scarcity.
We use the standards and guidelines of the Global Reporting Initiative, Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) industry specific standards and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) to inform our sustainability and related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports. The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.

**Quantitative Report for PG as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- Revenue: TTM revenue of $83.93B. Quarterly revenue showed growth from $19.38B (2022-03-31) to $21.44B (2023-12-31), with YoY increase of +3.2% in the latest quarter, but QoQ decrease of -2.0% from $21.87B.  
- Earnings: TTM net income of $14.87B. EPS diluted fluctuated: latest quarter $1.40 (YoY -11.9% from $1.59, QoQ -23.5% from $1.83).  
- Margins: Latest valuation ratios (2024-03-28) show net margin of 0.177, gross margin of 0.504, operating margin of 0.222. Computed quarterly margins vary; e.g., 2023-12-31 net margin ≈0.163 ($3.49B net income / $21.44B revenue) vs. 2023-09-30 ≈0.208 ($4.56B / $21.87B).  
- Cash-flow: No cash flow data available for any period.  
- Balance sheet: Total equity increased from $45.75B (2022-03-31) to $48.83B (2023-12-31). Total assets: $120.71B (2023-12-31) from $120.22B (2022-03-31). Total liabilities decreased from $74.47B to $71.88B over the same period.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- Earnings quality: Cash conversion unassessable due to no cash flow data. Net income volatility evident; e.g., QoQ net income decreased by -23.3% in latest quarter.  
- Balance-sheet resilience: Debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities/equity) improved from 1.63 in 2022-03-31 ($74.47B / $45.75B) to 1.47 in 2023-12-31 ($71.88B / $48.83B). Equity growth indicates strengthening resilience.

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**  
- Latest valuation (2024-03-28): P/E diluted of 26.942, P/B of 8.202, P/S of 4.772, earnings yield of 0.037.  
- Historical range (2023-04-03 to 2024-03-28): P/E diluted ranged 23.2 to 27.0; latest is near the high end. P/B ranged 7.4 to 8.5; latest is in the upper half. Net margin and ROE stable: net margin ranged 0.177–0.184, ROE ranged 0.304–0.321.

**4. Price Level and Returns versus Fundamentals**  
- Price: Close of $162.25 on 2024-03-28, with 52-week range $141.45 to $163.14.  
- Returns: Quarterly return (from 2023-12-27) of +11.1%, annual return (from 2023-03-28) of +10.9%.  
- Fundamental comparison: Annual price return of +10.9% vs. YoY EPS decline of -11.9%, indicating multiple expansion. Quarterly price gain of +11.1% vs. QoQ revenue decline of -2.0% and EPS decline of -23.5%, showing divergence.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- Upside: High ROE of 0.304 and revenue growth trajectory ($19.38B to $21.44B over 8 quarters) could support valuation if earnings recover. Balance sheet resilience with equity increase and liability decrease.  
- Downside: Elevated P/E of 26.942 relative to historical range; recent earnings weakness with EPS down YoY by -11.9%. Lack of cash flow data raises uncertainty. Price near 52-week high of $163.14, potential for correction.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
The stock trades at historically high valuations (P/E near 27) with recent price momentum diverging from weaker quarterly fundamentals (EPS down -23.5% QoQ). Balance-sheet resilience is positive (D/E of 1.47), but earnings quality is opaque without cash flow data. Upside is contingent on earnings improvement to justify valuation; downside risk from potential valuation compression if growth stalls. Quantitative indicators suggest a cautious stance due to valuation stretch and fundamental disconnect.
PG.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Report: Procter & Gamble (PG) as of 2024-04-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the 2023-08-04 10-K filing, Procter & Gamble is a global leader in branded consumer packaged goods, focused on delivering superior quality and value across approximately 180 countries. The business model is designed for balanced top- and bottom-line growth, relying on continuous innovation and productivity improvements. P&G's strategy centers on achieving meaningful superiority in product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution, and value. The company operates in highly competitive markets, competing against both other branded products and retailer private-label brands, and maintains leadership or significant share positions in its segments. Sales are diversified across numerous channels, with Walmart accounting for approximately 15% of total sales, and the top ten customers representing 40%.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Evidence)**
- **Earnings & Guidance (2024-03-25):** The company reported strong Q2-2024 results, posting net sales of $21.4 billion (up 3% YoY) and organic sales growth of 4%. Full-year guidance was raised, with core EPS growth guidance increased and the top-line outlook maintained. (Source: Web context, finance.yahoo.com)
- **Consumer Trend & Volume (2024-03-22):** The company faces short-term headwinds from a decrease in product volume, attributed to a consumer shift toward store-branded alternatives. (Source: Company news)
- **Dividend Outlook (2024-03-28):** P&G is cited as one of three dividend stocks likely to raise payouts in April 2024. (Source: Company news)
- **Corporate Social Responsibility (2023-08-04):** The company has a 2040 net zero emissions ambition and a Climate Transition Action Plan focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions. (Source: 10-K filing)

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*Confirmed Evidence:*
- **Risk: Competition from Private Labels & Consumer Shift (2024-03-22).** Reports indicate consumer shifts toward store-branded alternatives are causing a decrease in product volume, a key near-term pressure.
- **Risk: Regulatory and Legal Exposure.** The 10-K (2023-08-04) notes subjection to a wide spectrum of global regulations (FDA, EPA, FTC, GDPR, etc.). Specific legal actions cited include a 2024-02-12 lawsuit alleging discriminatory employment practices.
- **Offset: Pricing Power & Brand Strength.** The company's strategy and recent results (2024-03-25) indicate ability to drive organic growth, partly through price increases. The 10-K emphasizes marketing and R&D investment to support brand superiority.
- **Offset: Successful Legal Defense.** On 2024-03-25, P&G defeated class-action claims regarding its Cascade dishwasher pods. A separate false-advertising suit over melatonin was voluntarily dismissed with prejudice (2024-03-04).
- **Offset: Balanced Growth Model & Dividend Stature (2024-03-30, 2024-03-28).** Multiple news items highlight the company's stability and its consistent status as a dividend payer with expected near-term increases, framing it as a defensive investment.

*Inference:*
The tension between slowing volume growth (due to private-label competition) and resilient pricing power creates a key debate for the franchise. The company's long-term investment in branding and innovation (10-K) is the primary strategic asset meant to offset the inherent risk of consumer trade-down.

**4. Qualitative Assessment: Neutral**
The assessment is Neutral. The evidence presents a balanced view of a high-quality, defensive franchise with confirmed near-term headwinds (volume declines, consumer shifts) offset by evident strengths (pricing power, raised guidance, dividend growth, strong brand investment, manageable legal issues). The company is demonstrating operational resilience with organic sales growth and EPS guidance increases, but the narrative is tempered by the acknowledged challenge of slower expansion and competitive pressure from private labels, which constrain more bullish qualitative conclusions.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'net margin ranged 0.177–0.184' and 'ROE ranged 0.304–0.321' over the valuation window, but the raw valuation history shows net_margin values of 0.177, 0.178, 0.179, 0.18, 0.184 (five distinct values) and ROE values of 0.304, 0.313, 0.317, 0.319, 0.321. The ranges are directionally correct but the report's phrasing '0.2..0.2' and '0.3..0.3' in the raw pack summary is a rounding artifact; the quant report's stated ranges are consistent with the detailed data points. No material issue.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; ranges are supported by the detailed sparse history data.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'The company reported strong Q2-2024 results, posting net sales of $21.4 billion (up 3% YoY) and organic sales growth of 4%.' The raw quant data confirms revenue of $21.44B and YoY revenue growth of +3.2%, which is consistent. However, the qual report characterizes the results as 'strong' while the quant report highlights EPS declining -11.9% YoY and -23.5% QoQ. The qual report's 'strong' framing is sourced from the web context (finance.yahoo.com) and the company's own raised guidance, so it is not unsupported, but it downplays the earnings weakness that the quant report emphasizes.
corrections.required_action: PM should note the tension: qual 'strong' framing is company/analyst-sourced and does not account for the EPS decline visible in the quant data. Both reports are individually sourced but present a somewhat divergent tone.

corrections.issue: The qual report cites 'full-year guidance was raised, with core EPS growth guidance increased' from the 2024-03-25 web context. This is a forward-looking statement from the company. The quant report does not reference this guidance at all, which is appropriate since the quant pack contains no forward guidance data. No inconsistency, but the PM should be aware the qual's bullish tilt on guidance is not corroborated by backward-looking quant fundamentals.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed; the guidance claim is properly sourced to the web context. PM should weigh the forward guidance against the backward-looking EPS decline.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report's characterization of Q2-2024 results as 'strong' is sourced from web context but is not fully supported by the quant data, which shows EPS declining -11.9% YoY and -23.5% QoQ. The 'strong' label reflects the company's own framing and raised guidance, not the earnings trajectory visible in the raw data.

The quant report does not reference this guidance at all, which is appropriate since the quant pack contains no forward guidance data. No inconsistency, but the PM should be aware the qual's bullish tilt on guidance is not corroborated by backward-looking quant fundamentals. → No correction needed; the guidance claim is properly sourced to the web context. PM should weigh the forward guidance against the backward-looking EPS decline.

**Unsupported claims:**
- : The qual report's characterization of Q2-2024 results as 'strong' is sourced from web context but is not fully supported by the quant data, which shows EPS declining -11.9% YoY and -23.5% QoQ. The 'strong' label reflects the company's own framing and raised guidance, not the earnings trajectory visible in the raw data.

Recent earnings weakness: Latest quarter EPS of $1.40 declined -11.9% YoY and -23.5% QoQ, with net margin compressing to ~0.163 from ~0.208 in the prior quarter, reflecting volume headwinds from consumer shifts toward private-label alternatives (2024-03-22).

Balance sheet resilience and dividend: Debt-to-equity improved from 1.63 to 1.47 over 8 quarters, equity grew to $48.83B, and the company is expected to raise its dividend in April 2024 (2024-03-28), supporting the defensive investment thesis.

Forward guidance offset: Company raised full-year core EPS growth guidance on 2024-03-25 with organic sales growth of 4%, suggesting management confidence in earnings recovery, though this is forward-looking and not yet reflected in trailing fundamentals.

o and data communications services. We are licensed by the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to provide these wireless services on portions of the 800 MHz band, also known as cellular spectrum, the 1800-1900 MHz band, also known as Personal Communication Services (PCS) spectrum, portions of the 700 MHz upper C block, AWS 1 and 3 spectrum in the 1700 and 2100 MHz bands in areas that collectively cover nearly all of the population of the U.S., and 3700-3900 MHz, also known as C-Band. We have also deployed 4G technologies in 3.5 Gigahertz (GHz) shared spectrum, using LTE/Citizens Broadband Radio Service, and in 5 GHz unlicensed spectrum, using LTE/Licensed Assisted Access. All of this spectrum is collectively called low and mid-band spectrum. We are using our low and mid-band spectrum to provide 4G LTE and 5G wireless services. As of December 31, 2022, we retired our 3G service. We are increasingly reallocating spectrum previously used for 3G service to provide 4G LTE service, and we completed that transition where needed at the end of 2022. We are also utilizing additional low and mid-band spectrum for 5G through DSS to complement both our C-Band and our spectrum licenses in the 28 and 39 GHz band, collectively called millimeter wave spectrum.
Millimeter wave spectrum is being used in conjunction with low and mid-band spectrum for our 5G technology deployment. We own millimeter wave spectrum predominantly in the 28 GHz and 37/39 GHz bands. Millimeter wave spectrum is currently being used to increase capacity for mobile and fixed wireless services in areas of high demand. We anticipate that demand will continue to increase over time, driven by growth in customer connections and the increased usage of wireless broadband services that use more bandwidth and require faster rates of speed, as well as the wider deployment of 5G mobile and fixed services. We expect to meet the demand for 4G and 5G spectrum needs with our existing spectrum assets. If demand continues to increase or if new spectrum is required for a future generation of technology, we can meet that demand by acquiring licenses or leasing spectrum from other licensees, or by acquiring new spectrum licenses from the FCC, if and when future FCC spectrum auctions occur.
We use low and mid-band spectrum and DSS technology, to allow 5G service to run simultaneously with 4G LTE on multiple spectrum bands. With DSS, whenever customers move outside Verizon's millimeter wave and C-Band coverage areas, their 5G-enabled devices will remain on 5G technology using the lower spectrum bands where this network is available. This allows us to more fully and effectively utilize our current spectrum resources to serve both 4G and 5G customers.
From time to time we have exchanged spectrum licenses with other wireless service providers through secondary market swap transactions. We expect to continue to pursue similar opportunities to trade spectrum licenses in order to meet capacity and expansion needs in the future. In certain cases, we have entered into intra-market spectrum swaps designed to increase the amount of contiguous spectrum within frequency bands in a specific market. Contiguous spectrum improves network performance and efficiency. These swaps, as well as any spectrum purchases, require us to obtain governmental approvals.

Competition and Related Trends
The telecommunications industry is highly competitive. The rapid development of new technologies, services and products has eliminated many of the traditional distinctions among wireless, cable, internet and local and long distance communication services and brought new competitors to our markets. We expect competition to remain intense as traditional and non-traditional participants seek increased market share.
With respect to our wireless connectivity products and services, we compete against other national wireless service providers, including AT&T Inc. and T-Mobile US, Inc., as well as various regional wireless service providers. We also compete for retail activations with resellers that buy bulk wholesale service from wireless service providers, including Verizon, and resell it to their customers. Resellers include cable companies, such as Comcast Corporation and Charter Communications, Inc., and others. Competition remains intense as a result of various factors, including high rates of smartphone penetration in the wireless market, increased network investment by our competitors, the development and deployment of new technologies, including 5G, the introduction of new products and services, offerings that include additional bundled premium content, increased levels of promotions and service plan discounts, new market entrants, the availability of additional licensed and unlicensed spectrum and regulatory changes. In addition, increasing government incentives related to network deployment may enhance the ability of certain of our competitors to compete with us. Competition may also increase as smaller, stand-alone wireless service providers merge or transfer licenses to larger, better capitalized wireless service providers and as MVNOs resell wireless communication services. In addition, DISH Network Corporation has committed to deploy a facilities-based 5G broadband network in each of its license areas capable of serving at least 70% of the U.S. population by June 2023, which could result in additional competitive pressures in the U.S. wireless industry.
We also face competition from other communications and technology companies seeking to increase their brand recognition and capture customer revenue with respect to the provision of wireless products and services, in addition to non-traditional offerings in mobile data. For example, Microsoft Corporation, Alphabet Inc., Apple Inc., Meta Platforms, Inc. and others are offering alternative means for messaging and making wireless voice calls that, in certain cases, can be used in lieu of the wireless providers' voice service, as well as alternative means of accessing video content. In addition, we expect to see increasing competition in the provisioning of internet access by low Earth orbit satellite companies as well in the area of fixed wireless offerings that provide an alternative to traditional landline internet service providers.
With respect to our wireline connectivity services, we compete against cable companies, wireless service providers, domestic and foreign telecommunications providers, satellite television companies, internet service providers, over-the-top (OTT) providers and other companies that offer network services and managed enterprise solutions. We also face increasing competition from other providers of VoIP services as well as internet portal providers. Cable operators have increased the size and capacity of their networks in order to deliver digital products and services. Several major cable operators offer bundles with wireless services through strategic relationships. Traditional wireless carriers are also bundling broadband internet offerings with wireless services while increasing their broadband internet footprint. Customers have an increasing number of choices for obtaining video content from various online services. We expect the market will continue to shift from traditional linear video to OTT offerings. We also expect customer migration from traditional voice services to wireless services to continue as a growing number of customers place greater value on mobility and wireless companies position their services as a landline alternative.
We believe that the following are the most important competitive factors and trends in the telecommunications industry:
- Network reliability, speed, capacity and coverage.

VZ.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: Verizon (VZ) as of 2024-04-01

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** TTM revenue is $133.97B. Quarterly revenue was stable ($32.60B-$35.25B) over 8 quarters, with a QoQ increase of +5.4% in Q4 2023 but a marginal YoY decline of -0.3%.
- **Earnings:** TTM net income is $12.10B. The quarterly trajectory shows a sharp Q4 2023 divergence: net income was -$2.57B (EPS -$0.65) versus a prior 4-quarter average of ~$5.15B. This loss drove a QoQ EPS decline of -157.5% and a YoY decline of -141.4%.
- **Margins:** Q4 2023 operating income was $600M, yielding an operating margin of ~1.7% (operating income/revenue). This is a severe drop from the prior three-quarter average operating income of ~$7.4B (margin ~22.5%).
- **Cash Flow:** Cash flow statement data is unavailable for all periods supplied.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity stood at $93.80B at end-2023, down from $99.09B at end-Q3 2023 but up from $92.46B at end-2022. Total assets were $380.25B, slightly above the 2022 year-end level of $379.68B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** The Q4 2023 EPS of -$0.65 represents a significant negative outlier against a stable prior quarterly range of $1.09-$1.57. The resulting TTM EPS (approximated from TTM net income / Q4 diluted shares) suggests a sharp quarterly deterioration, likely from non-recurring charges, which reduces the reliability of the latest period's reported earnings.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities / total equity) was 3.05x ($286.46B / $93.80B) at Q4 2023, up from 2.89x ($287.22B / $92.46B) at YE 2022. Leverage increased slightly over the year.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
- **P/E (Diluted):** The latest P/E of 15.26 (2024-03-28) is at the absolute top of its 365-day range (6.0 – 15.3) and has doubled from its value of ~8.15 on 2024-01-24. This expansion occurred despite the recent negative earnings quarter, indicating valuation is decoupled from trailing earnings.
- **P/B:** The latest P/B of 1.886 is also near the top of its range (1.3 – 1.9).
- **Implied Metrics:** The net margin (0.09) and ROE (0.129) as of 2024-03-28 are half their levels from one year prior (~0.159, ~0.235).

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
- **Price & Returns:** The stock closed at $41.96 on 2024-03-28. It returned **+12.5%** over the prior quarter (from $37.31) and **+10.4%** annually (from $38.00). The 52-week trading range is $30.14 - $43.21.
- **Path:** The price has risen sharply (+15.2% from Q3 to Q4 2023) immediately following the quarter that produced a large earnings loss.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** The TTM net income of $12.10B remains positive, indicating prior profitability. If the Q4 2023 loss is confirmed as a large one-time charge and future earnings revert toward the prior $5B quarterly run-rate, the current elevated P/E could compress rapidly on improved trailing earnings.
- **Downside:** The leverage ratio (liabilities/equity) of 3.05x is high. The consensus valuation expansion (P/E moving from ~8x to ~15x) occurred alongside deteriorating reported margins and ROE, creating a disconnect. A reversion of the P/E toward its 12-month average (~8.5x) on continued weak earnings would imply substantial downside from current price levels.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental trajectory deteriorated sharply in Q4 2023, with a net loss and collapsing margins. Concurrently, the valuation multiple (P/E, P/B) expanded to the top of its one-year range, and the stock price rallied. This creates a tension: the elevated valuation is not supported by the latest earnings report. The risk is a correction as the market reconciles the optimistic price move with the poor fundamental print. The prospective reward hinges on the unconfirmed thesis that Q4 earnings are non-repeating. **The quantitative evidence presents a high-risk profile, with the stock priced at a premium to its recent fundamental history while key quality metrics (margin, ROE, earnings stability) have weakened.**
VZ.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Analysis: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ)

**1. Business / Franchise Description**
From the 10-K filing (2023-02-10), Verizon is described as a leading provider of communications, technology, information, and entertainment products and services. It operates through two segments: Verizon Consumer Group (76% of 2022 revenue) and Verizon Business Group (23% of 2022 revenue). The Consumer segment offers wireless (postpaid and prepaid) and wireline services (FiOS, FWA, DSL) to consumers. The Business segment provides similar services plus networking, security, IoT, and managed solutions to businesses, government, and carriers. The company emphasizes its extensive network (4G LTE covering ~328 million POPs, 5G C-Band covering ~189 million POPs as of 2022), reliability, and growth in fixed wireless access (FWA). Key competitive factors cited include network reliability, speed, capacity, and coverage.

**2. Recent Developments (Point-in-Time: as of 2024-04-01)**
*   **Corporate Actions & Strategy (2024-03-28):** Verizon announced it joined RE100, a global initiative committed to 100% renewable electricity, as part of its Responsible Business Plan.
*   **Earnings Guidance (2024-03-26, 2024-03-19):** Verizon confirmed it will report Q1 2024 earnings on April 22, 2024. Web context notes consensus estimates for Q1 2024 EPS of $1.12 (down from $1.20 YoY) and revenue of ~$33.44B (+1.6% YoY).
*   **Product & Partnerships (2024-03-27):** Verizon made its "Intelligent Call Back" capabilities, powered by Mindful by Medallia, available on California's CALNET contract vehicle for state agencies.
*   **Marketing (2024-03-27):** Verizon launched a social media sweepstakes offering fans a chance to attend the LALIGA "ElClásico" match in Spain.
*   **Promotions (2024-03-26):** A telecoms advertisement noted a promotion: "get a Galaxy S23 with Galaxy AI on us with a new line on Verizon myPlan."
*   **Enterprise Growth Catalyst (2024-03-05, Web Context):** An analyst note suggested Verizon's focus on reliability could help it build a dominant position in enterprise private 5G multi-access edge computing (MEC), citing its head start in individualized private MECs.
*   **Subscriber Growth Catalyst (2024-02-15, Web Context):** A news report highlighted that Verizon's fixed wireless subscriber growth reached over three million, a 31% increase over 2022.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk - Litigation & Regulatory:** Multiple class-action lawsuits were filed in February 2024 alleging data breaches, deceptive billing practices (administrative charges), and failure to protect employee data (2024-02-15, 2024-02-22, 2024-02-23). A proposed $100 million settlement for a prior billing suit is also noted (2024-02-15). These pose financial, reputational, and regulatory risks.
*   **Risk - Competition:** The 10-K cites intense competition from national carriers (AT&T, T-Mobile), cable MVNOs, and potential new entrants (e.g., DISH). Non-traditional competitors offer alternative messaging and data services. In wireline, competition is strong from cable operators bundling wireless.
*   **Risk - Technology & Capital Intensity:** Continuous investment in network evolution (5G, fiber) is required. The 10-K notes the need for significant site rights and government approvals for network build-out, creating execution risk.
*   **Offset - Brand & Network Strength:** The business narrative consistently emphasizes network reliability, speed, and coverage as core competitive advantages. The FWA subscriber growth (31% YoY as of 2024-02-15) demonstrates traction in a key growth area.
*   **Offset - Enterprise & Public Sector Positioning:** Insight from web context (2024-03-05) suggests a potential early-mover advantage in private 5G MEC solutions for enterprises. The firm's established relationships with government agencies (e.g., via CALNET contract, 2024-03-27) provide a stable revenue base in the Public Sector segment.
*   **Offset - Shareholder Return Focus:** News coverage and analyst commentary consistently highlight Verizon as a high-yield dividend stock, which can attract income-seeking investors.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**

**Neutral.**

**Rationale:** The assessment is tempered by significant legal headwinds and persistent competitive pressures, which are clearly evidenced in the recent narrative. While the Q1 2024 earnings preview suggests stable, modest revenue growth, the expected year-over-year decline in EPS points to cost or margin challenges. Offsets include VZ's strong brand, network infrastructure, demonstrated growth in fixed wireless access, and its entrenched position in government and enterprise markets. The seniority and scale of its franchise provide stability, but near-term sentiment may be weighed by litigation costs and competitive intensity. The balance of confirmed positive trends and confirmed risks results in a neutral qualitative outlook based on the provided point-in-time evidence.

The report correctly notes cash flow unavailability but does not flag that the TTM operating income figure ($22.88B) is available and could be used as a partial proxy for cash generation capacity, which is relevant given the high leverage discussion.
corrections.required_action: No action required — the report accurately states cash flow unavailability. Minor note: TTM operating income of $22.88B is present in the growth panel and could have been referenced more explicitly when discussing balance-sheet resilience, but this is a stylistic point, not an error.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'The expected year-over-year decline in EPS points to cost or margin challenges' regarding Q1 2024 consensus EPS of $1.12 vs $1.20 YoY. This is sourced from web context (analyst consensus) and is consistent with the supplied evidence. However, the qual report does not explicitly connect this to the Q4 2023 operating income collapse (from ~$7.4B to $600M) documented in the quant pack, which would strengthen the margin-challenge narrative.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed — the claim is supported by web context. The omission of the Q4 2023 operating income detail is a missed connection, not an unsupported claim.

corrections.issue: Quant report states the P/E 'doubled from its value of ~8.15 on 2024-01-24.' The raw evidence shows pe_diluted=8.145 on 2024-01-24 and 15.258 on 2024-03-28. 15.258/8.145 = 1.87x, not exactly 2x. 'Doubled' is a slight overstatement (it increased ~87%).
corrections.required_action: PM should note the P/E expansion was ~87% (from 8.1x to 15.3x), not a full doubling. The directional point — that P/E expanded dramatically alongside deteriorating fundamentals — remains valid.

unsupported_claims: : The quant report's claim that P/E 'doubled' from ~8.15 to 15.26 is a ~87% increase, not a 100% increase. This is a minor overstatement but does not change the directional conclusion.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Verizon's Q4 2023 earnings collapsed to a net loss of -$2.57B with operating margins falling to ~1.7%, yet the stock rallied 12.5% in the prior quarter and the P/E expanded ~87% to 15.3x — the top of its one-year range. This valuation-fundamental disconnect, combined with elevated leverage at 3.05x debt-to-equity and multiple class-action lawsuits filed in February 2024, creates a mildly unfavorable risk/reward profile over the next year.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Verizon's Q4 2023 earnings collapsed to a net loss of -$2.57B with operating margins falling to ~1.7%, yet the stock rallied 12.5% in the prior quarter and the P/E expanded ~87% to 15.3x — the top of its one-year range. This valuation-fundamental disconnect, combined with elevated leverage at 3.05x debt-to-equity and multiple class-action lawsuits filed in February 2024, creates a mildly unfavorable risk/reward profile over the next year.
key_factors: Q4 2023 net income was -$2.57B (EPS -$0.65) versus a prior 4-quarter average of ~$5.15B, with operating margins collapsing to ~1.7% from a prior three-quarter average of ~22.5%, signaling a severe but potentially non-recurring earnings deterioration.

P/E expanded ~87% from 8.1x (2024-01-24) to 15.3x (2024-03-28) — the absolute top of its 365-day range — while net margin halved to 0.09 and ROE fell to 0.129, creating a valuation-fundamental disconnect.

Debt-to-equity ratio of 3.05x at Q4 2023 increased from 2.89x at YE 2022, and multiple class-action lawsuits were filed in February 2024 alleging data breaches and deceptive billing, adding financial and reputational risk.

Fixed wireless access subscriber growth of 31% YoY reaching over 3 million subscribers and consensus Q1 2024 revenue growth of +1.6% YoY provide modest fundamental offsets but are insufficient to justify the elevated multiple.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-07-20 section=business period_end=2023-05-31
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
NIKE, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 under the laws of the State of Oregon. As used in this Annual Report on Form 10-K (this "Annual Report"), the terms "we," "us," "our," "NIKE" and the "Company" refer to NIKE, Inc. and its predecessors, subsidiaries and affiliates, collectively, unless the context indicates otherwise.
Our principal business activity is the design, development and worldwide marketing and selling of athletic footwear, apparel, equipment, accessories and services. NIKE is the largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel in the world. We sell our products through NIKE Direct operations, which are comprised of both NIKE-owned retail stores and sales through our digital platforms (also referred to as "NIKE Brand Digital"), to retail accounts and to a mix of independent distributors, licensees and sales representatives in nearly all countries around the world. We also offer interactive consumer services and experiences through our digital platforms. Nearly all of our products are manufactured by independent contractors. Nearly all footwear and apparel products are manufactured outside the United States, while equipment products are manufactured both in the United States and abroad.
All references to fiscal 2023, 2022, 2021 and 2020 are to NIKE, Inc.'s fiscal years ended May 31, 2023, 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. Any references to other fiscal years refer to a fiscal year ending on May 31 of that year.
PRODUCTS
Our NIKE Brand product offerings are aligned around our consumer construct focused on Men's, Women's and Kids'. We also design products specifically for the Jordan Brand and Converse. We believe this approach allows us to create products that better meet individual consumer needs while accelerating our largest growth opportunities.
NIKE's athletic footwear products are designed primarily for specific athletic use, although a large percentage of the products are worn for casual or leisure purposes. We place considerable emphasis on innovation and high-quality construction in the development and manufacturing of our products. Our Men's, Women's and Jordan Brand footwear products currently lead in footwear sales and we expect them to continue to do so.
We also sell sports apparel, which features the same trademarks and are sold predominantly through the same marketing and distribution channels as athletic footwear. Our sports apparel, similar to our athletic footwear products, is designed primarily for athleti
…[truncated 19516 chars]…
nd obtained trademark registrations.
We have copyright protection in our designs, graphics, software applications, digital goods and other original works. When appropriate, we also obtain registered copyrights.
We file for, own and maintain many U.S. and foreign utility and design patents protecting components, technologies, materials, manufacturing techniques, features, functionality, and industrial designs used in and for the manufacture of various athletic, performance, and leisure footwear and apparel, including physical and digital versions thereof, athletic equipment, and digital devices, and related software applications. These patents expire at various times.
We believe our success depends upon our capabilities in areas such as design, research and development, production and marketing and is supported and protected by our intellectual property rights, such as trademarks, utility and design patents, copyrights, and trade secrets, among others.
We have followed a policy of applying for and registering intellectual property rights in the United States and select foreign countries on trademarks, inventions, innovations and designs that we deem valuable. We also continue to vigorously protect our intellectual property, including trademarks, patents and trade secrets against third-party infringement and misappropriation.
2023 FORM 10-K   5
Table of Contents
HUMAN CAPITAL RESOURCES
At NIKE, we consider the strength and effective management of our workforce to be essential to the ongoing success of our business. We believe that it is important to attract, develop and retain a diverse and engaged workforce at all levels of our business and that such a workforce fosters creativity and accelerates innovation. We are focused on building an increasingly diverse talent pipeline that reflects our consumers, athletes and the communities we serve.
CULTURE
Each employee shapes NIKE's culture through behaviors and practices. This starts with our Maxims, which represent our core values and, along with our Code of Conduct, feature the fundamental behaviors that help anchor, inform and guide us and apply to all employees. Our mission is to bring inspiration and innovation to every athlete in the world, which includes the belief that if you have a body, you are an athlete. We aim to do this by creating groundbreaking sport innovations, making our products more sustainably, building a creative and diverse global team, supporting the well-being of our employees and making a positive impact in communities where we live and work. Our mission is aligned with our deep commitment to maintaining an environment where all NIKE employees have the opportunity to reach their full potential, to connect to our brands and to shape our workplace culture. We believe providing for growth and retention of our employees is essential in fostering such a culture and are dedicated to giving access to training programs and career development opportunities, including trainings on NIKE's values, history and business, trainings on developing leadership skills at all levels, tools and resources for managers and qualified tuition reimbursement opportunities.
As part of our commitment to empowering our employees to help shape our culture, we source employee feedback through our Engagement Survey program, including several corporate pulse surveys. The program provides every employee throughout the globe an opportunity to provide confidential feedback on key areas known to drive employee engagement, including their satisfaction with their managers, their work and the Company generally. The program also measures our employees' emotional commitment to NIKE as well as NIKE's culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. NIKE also provides multiple points of contact for employees to speak up if they experience something that does not align with our values or otherwise violates our workplace policies, even if they are uncertain what they observed or heard is a violation of company policy.
As part of our commitment to make a positive impact on our communities, we maintain a goal of investing 2% of our prior fiscal year's pre-tax income into global communities. The focus of this investment continues to be inspiring kids to be active through play and sport as well as uniting and inspiring communities to create a better and more equitable future for all. Our community investments are an important part of our culture in that we also support employees in giving back to community organizations through donations and volunteering, which are matched by the NIKE Foundation where eligible.
EMPLOYEE BASE
As of May 31, 2023, we had approximately 83,700 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees. We also utilize independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce.
None of our employees are represented by a union, except certain employees in the EMEA and APLA geographies are members of and/or represented by trade unions, as allowed or required by local law and/or collective bargaining agreements. Also, in some countries outside of the United States, local laws require employee representation by works councils (which may be entitled to information and consultation on certain subsidiary decisions) or by organizations similar to a union. In certain European countries, we are required by local law to enter into, and/or comply with, industry-wide or national collective bargaining agreements. NIKE has never experienced a material interruption of operations due to labor disagreements.
DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION
Diversity, equity and inclusion ("DE&I") is a strategic priority for NIKE and we are committed to having an increasingly diverse team and culture. We aim to foster an inclusive and accessible workplace through recruitment, development and retention of diverse talent with the goal of expanding representation across all dimensions of diversity over the long term. We remain committed to the targets announced in fiscal 2021 for the Company to work toward by fiscal 2025, including increasing representation of women in our global corporate workforce and leadership positions, as well as increasing representation of U.S. racial and ethnic minorities in our U.S. corporate workforce and at the Director level and above.
We continue to enhance our efforts to recruit diverse talent through our traditional channels and through initiatives, such as partnerships with athletes and sports-related organizations to create apprenticeship programs and new partnerships with organizations, colleges and universities that serve diverse populations. Additionally, we are prioritizing DE&I education so that all NIKE employees and leaders have the cultural awareness and understanding to lead inclusively and build diverse and inclusive teams. We also have Employee Networks, collectively known as NikeUNITED, representing various employee groups.

NKE.quantitative_report:
**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**

- **Revenue**: TTM revenue as of Q2 2023 (2023-11-30) is $51.54B. Quarterly revenue shows a strong QoQ rebound in Q2 (+3.5% to $13.39B) but modest YoY growth (+0.5% vs. 2022-11-30).
- **Earnings**: TTM net income is $5.30B. Quarterly EPS diluted has recovered from a trough of $0.66 (Q4 2022-05-31) to $1.03 (Q2 2023-11-30), a YoY increase of +21.2%.
- **Margins**: TTM operating margin is 12.2%. Quarterly operating margin recovered to 14.3% in Q2 2023-11-30 (from 9.7% in Q4 2022-05-31). TTM gross margin is 44.0%.
- **Cash-Flow**: Free cash flow data is unavailable for the entire 7-quarter period.
- **Balance-Sheet**: Total equity has declined from $15.28B (Q4 2022-05-31) to $14.15B (Q2 2023-11-30). Total assets have decreased from $40.32B to $37.20B over the same period. Debt reported at $8.93B as of Q4 2022-05-31.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**

- **Earnings Quality**: The strong YoY EPS growth (+21.2%) is primarily driven by margin expansion (operating margin recovery from 10.2% a year prior to 14.3%) rather than robust revenue growth (+0.5% YoY). The TTM net margin of 10.3% is stable with the recent quarter.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Total equity as a percentage of total assets has remained relatively stable, declining slightly from 37.9% (Q4 2022-05-31) to 38.0% (Q2 2023-11-30). The absolute level of net equity has decreased. Detailed debt and current ratio data are unavailable in the provided statements.

**3. Valuation versus the Company's Own Supplied History**

- The latest P/E diluted of 27.172 (2024-03-28) is at the low end of its 365-day range (26.8 to 37.5). It has declined steadily from 33.957 in April 2023.
- The latest P/B of 10.179 is also at the low end of its 365-day range (10.0 to 13.8).
- Net margin has been stable at 10.3% for the last four data points, down from 11.5% in early 2023. ROE is stable at 37.5%.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus the Fundamental Path**

- The stock price of $93.98 (2024-03-28) is near its 52-week low of $88.66 and significantly below its 52-week high of $128.68.
- The stock has underperformed fundamentals: despite improving quarterly earnings (EPS from $0.85 to $1.03 YoY), the price is down -20.3% annually (from $117.87 on 2023-03-28).
- Quarterly price momentum is negative at -12.3% (from $107.13 on 2023-12-27), following a sharp prior-quarter rally of +19.8%.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**

- **Upside**: Valuation multiples are at the low end of their 1-year ranges (P/E 27.2x vs. range 26.8-37.5x; P/B 10.2x vs. range 10.0-13.8x). Earnings trajectory is positive, with quarterly EPS expanding from a trough. A return of the valuation multiple toward its 1-year mean (P/E ~31) would imply significant price appreciation if earnings are sustained.
- **Downside**: Revenue growth is anaemic at +0.5% YoY. The stock's recent price action (-20.3% annual) demonstrates multiple compression despite earnings growth, indicating persistent market skepticism. A further contraction of the P/E multiple or a reversion in the recent margin recovery poses downside risk.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**

The quantitative profile is mixed. Weak top-line growth (+0.5% YoY) is offset by a recovery in margins and earnings, leading to strong EPS growth (+21.2% YoY). Valuations have compressed to the low end of the 1-year range (P/E 27.2x, P/B 10.2x). However, the stock's price has significantly lagged its earnings improvement, resulting in a -20.3% annual return. The primary quantitative upside is from valuation mean-reversion; the primary downside risk is from continued multiple compression or a stall in the earnings recovery. The balance sheet shows stable leverage but declining equity. The absence of cash flow data limits a full assessment. The risk/reward appears contingent on market willingness to re-rate the multiple lower from already depressed levels versus a continuation of the fundamental earnings recovery.
NKE.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Report: Nike (NKE) as of 2024-04-01

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Nike is the world's largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel, operating a vertically integrated brand model focused on innovation, marketing, and direct consumer engagement. Its franchise includes the core Nike brand, Jordan, and Converse, distributed through owned retail, digital platforms (NIKE Direct), and wholesale channels globally. Manufacturing is almost entirely outsourced to independent contractors primarily in Vietnam, Indonesia, and China. The company emphasizes intellectual property protection and responds to consumer trends through design, athlete endorsements, and digital experiences.

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
- **2024-03-27**: Nike's Air Max DN launch on "Air Max Day" was successful, viewed as a positive catalyst addressing concerns about product innovation. An analyst reiterated an Outperform rating with a $115 price target, citing the launch's favorable reception.
- **2024-03-22**: In its earnings call, Nike reported slight revenue growth and acknowledged near-term headwinds but reiterated its commitment to innovation-driven long-term growth and elevating the consumer experience.
- **2024-03-22**: Goldman Sachs lowered its stock target to $120 but maintained a Buy rating, citing potential for cost recovery, strengthening inventory conditions, and anticipated acceleration of innovation.
- **2024-03-20**: An earnings preview indicated management is laying groundwork for product launches to deliver top-line growth acceleration in the second half of 2024 and into 2025.
- **2024-03-29**: Foot Locker stock surged on speculation that Nike could come to the retailer's rescue, suggesting potential strategic partnership or support.

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
- **Competition**: The athletic footwear and apparel industry is highly competitive, with rivals including adidas, Lululemon, New Balance, and others (10-K, 2023-07-20).
- **Manufacturing Concentration**: Nearly all production is outsourced to independent manufacturers in Vietnam (~50% footwear), Indonesia, and China, exposing Nike to geopolitical, trade, and supply chain disruptions (10-K, 2023-07-20).
- **Consumer Sensitivity**: Demand is affected by changing preferences, economic conditions, and macroeconomic uncertainty; struggles of consumer-facing companies were noted in broader market commentary (2024-03-31).
- **Legal Proceedings**: Ongoing trademark infringement lawsuits (e.g., against BAPE) involve legal costs and management attention, though they aim to protect IP (multiple articles, 2024-03-06 to 2024-03-11).
- **Trade Protectionism**: International operations face risks from tariffs, anti-dumping measures, and other trade restrictions (10-K, 2023-07-20).

**Offsets:**
- **Brand Strength & Innovation**: Strong global brand recognition, a robust intellectual property portfolio, and a pipeline of innovative technologies (e.g., Air Max, Flyknit) support pricing power and consumer appeal (10-K, 2023-07-20).
- **Direct-to-Consumer & Digital Focus**: NIKE Direct operations and digital platforms enhance margin and consumer engagement, with investments in digital services and experiences (10-K, 2023-07-20).
- **Supply Chain Flexibility**: While concentrated, Nike states it can develop alternative sourcing over time and has actively managed trade issues (10-K, 2023-07-20).
- **Recent Product Momentum**: The successful Air Max DN launch and management's focus on product launches signal potential near-term growth catalysts (2024-03-27, 2024-03-20).

#### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** Nike's franchise remains dominant, and recent product launches offer positive momentum. However, near-term headwinds from competition, macroeconomic pressures, legal actions, and supply chain risks temper the outlook. The balance of strong brand equity against these challenges results in a neutral qualitative view.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Debt reported at $8.93B as of Q4 2022-05-31' but the raw balance sheet shows total_debt=8.93B at 2023-05-31 (Q4 FY2023), not Q4 2022-05-31. The report also says 'Total equity has declined from $15.28B (Q4 2022-05-31) to $14.15B (Q2 2023-11-30)' — the raw data shows total_equity=15.28B at 2022-05-31 and 14.15B at 2023-11-30, so the equity figures are correct but the debt date label is wrong (should be 2023-05-31, not 2022-05-31).
corrections.required_action: Correct the debt date reference from 'Q4 2022-05-31' to 'Q4 2023-05-31' to match the raw balance sheet data.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin has been stable at 10.3% for the last four data points, down from 11.5% in early 2023.' The raw valuation history shows net_margin=0.115 on 2023-04-03, then 0.108 for several points, then 0.099, then 0.098, then 0.103 for the last three points (2024-01-24 through 2024-03-28). So net margin was 10.3% for only the last 3 data points, not 4, and the decline was from 11.5% to 9.8% before recovering to 10.3%. The characterization of 'stable at 10.3% for the last four data points' is inaccurate.
corrections.required_action: Correct the net margin narrative to reflect the actual trajectory: declined from 11.5% to ~9.8%, then recovered to 10.3% over the last three data points.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'ROE is stable at 37.5%.' The raw data shows roe=0.375 for the last three points but roe=0.362 for the four points from 2023-08-11 through 2023-12-19, and roe=0.369-0.377 earlier. So ROE is not simply 'stable at 37.5%' — it dipped to 36.2% for several quarters before recovering. This is a minor overstatement.
corrections.required_action: Note that ROE recovered from 36.2% to 37.5% rather than being flat at 37.5%.

unsupported_claims: The quant report's claim 'Debt reported at $8.93B as of Q4 2022-05-31' is inconsistent with raw data showing total_debt=8.93B at 2023-05-31 (Q4 FY2023). The date is mislabeled.

The quant report's claim 'Net margin has been stable at 10.3% for the last four data points' is not supported by the raw valuation history, which shows 0.098 for 2023-10-16 through 2023-12-19 and 0.103 only for the last three points.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Nike's valuation has compressed to the low end of its 1-year range (P/E 27.2x vs. 26.8-37.5x) while earnings are recovering (+21.2% YoY EPS growth), but anaemic revenue growth (+0.5% YoY) and persistent market skepticism reflected in -20% annual price decline create a balanced risk/reward with limited near-term catalysts for re-rating.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Nike's valuation has compressed to the low end of its 1-year range (P/E 27.2x vs. 26.8-37.5x) while earnings are recovering (+21.2% YoY EPS growth), but anaemic revenue growth (+0.5% YoY) and persistent market skepticism reflected in -20% annual price decline create a balanced risk/reward with limited near-term catalysts for re-rating.
key_factors: Valuation at low end of 1-year range: P/E of 27.2x (vs. range 26.8-37.5x) and P/B of 10.2x (vs. range 10.0-13.8x) as of 2024-03-28, offering potential mean-reversion upside if earnings trajectory sustains.

Earnings recovery driven by margin expansion: quarterly operating margin recovered to 14.3% in Q2 2023-11-30 from 9.7% trough, with EPS up +21.2% YoY to $1.03, though this masks only +0.5% YoY revenue growth indicating limited top-line momentum.

Stock has significantly underperformed fundamentals: -20.3% annual return (from $117.87 to $93.98) despite improving earnings, reflecting persistent multiple compression and market skepticism about Nike's growth trajectory.

Recent product innovation catalysts: Air Max DN launch on 2024-03-27 received positive reception, and management signaled product launches to drive growth acceleration in H2 2024 and 2025, though execution remains unproven.

# UNH Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2024-04-01

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** grew from a quarterly run-rate of ~$80.15B (Q1 2022) to $94.43B (Q4 2023), representing +17.8% over eight quarters. YoY growth in the latest quarter was +14.1%. TTM revenue stands at $371.62B. Sequentially, Q4 2023 revenue grew only +2.2% QoQ over Q3 2023 ($92.36B), indicating deceleration from the prior year's pace.

**Earnings**: TTM net income is $23.14B. Quarterly EPS rose from $5.03 (Q4 2022) to $5.85 (Q4 2023), +16.3% YoY. However, the most recent quarter showed a sequential EPS decline from $6.24 (Q3 2023) to $5.85, a −6.3% QoQ drop alongside +2.2% revenue growth—implying margin compression in the latest period.

**Margins**: Latest-quarter gross margin is 89.2% (gross profit $84.23B / revenue $94.43B). Operating margin compressed to 8.1% in Q4 2023 ($7.69B operating income), down from 9.2% in Q3 ($8.53B/$92.36B). Net margin for Q4 was 6.0% ($5.67B/$94.43B), also below Q3's 6.5%. TTM operating income totals $32.36B.

**Cash flow**: All quarterly cash-flow data are unavailable. Free-cash-flow trajectory and TTM FCF cannot be assessed.

**Balance sheet**: Total equity expanded from $81.45B (Q4 2022) to $94.42B (Q4 2023), +15.9% YoY. Total liabilities stood at $174.80B at Q4 2023, yielding a liabilities-to-equity ratio of 1.85x. Total assets are $273.72B. The equity base has grown steadily each quarter from $76.13B (Q1 2022), with a notable step-up of ~$4.9B in Q4 2023 alone ($89.56B → $94.42B), suggesting a period of equity accumulation or net income retention.

## 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality**: The high and stable gross margin (~89%) reflects a low cost-of-revenue structure ($10.19B of $94.43B revenue in Q4). The narrowing occurs at operating level: operating margin at 8.1% in Q4 versus a range of 7.6–9.2% over the trailing eight quarters, suggesting operating cost variability. Net margin has compressed slightly YoY (from ~6.0% in Q4 2022 to 6.0% in Q4 2023, but down from 6.5% in Q3 2023). ROE (TTM) is reported at 24.5%, modestly below its recent range peak of 25.3% (April 2023). With no cash-flow data, operating-to-earnings conversion quality cannot be evaluated.

**Balance-sheet resilience**: The liabilities-to-equity ratio of 1.85x indicates moderate leverage for a managed-care/health-services firm. Equity has grown consistently, from $76.13B → $94.42B over the eight-quarter period, an addition of $18.3B. The balance sheet expanded by $52.5B in total assets over this period (from $221.24B to $273.72B), suggesting significant growth in both operations and potentially investment assets. Without a breakdown of asset composition and liability maturity, the precise resilience profile is limited.

## 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

**P/E**: The latest diluted P/E is 20.73 (2024-03-28), at the lower end of its supplied 12-point range (19.8–24.8). Over the supplied history, P/E declined from 23.33 (April 2023) to 20.73 (March 2024), a 11.1% compression. The current multiple is 13.0% below the range midpoint of ~22.3.

**P/B**: At 4.91, the latest P/B is at the absolute low of the 12-point range (4.7–6.1), and down from 5.76 at the start of the window.

**P/S**: 1.249 on a TTM revenue base of $371.62B.

**Earnings yield**: 4.8% at the latest reading.

**Net margin**: Stable at 6.2% throughout most of the supplied period, with a slight tick to 6.2% and ROE at 24.5% at the latest point.

In summary, valuation multiples have compressed meaningfully over the past year while trailing fundamentals (EPS, revenue, equity) have grown. The P/E at 20.73 and P/B at 4.91 sit near or below the lower end of the one-year range, implying the market is pricing in either the margin compression signal in Q4, decelerating revenue growth, or broader sector rotation.

## 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context

**Current price**: $494.70 (2024-03-28).

**52-week range**: High of $554.70, low of $445.68. The stock sits at the 46th percentile of this range, roughly mid-range but trending lower: -10.8% from the 52-week high.

**Quarterly return (Q1 2024)**: −5.4% (from $522.79 on 2023-12-27 to $494.70), against modest fundamental improvement (revenue +2.2% QoQ but EPS −6.3% QoQ).

**Annual return**: +4.7% (from $472.58 on 2023-03-28), significantly trailing the EPS growth of +16.3% YoY. This disparity, combined with P/E compression from ~23.3 to ~20.7, indicates the market has derated the stock despite strong fundamental growth.

**Quarterly path**: The stock rose +0.4% in Q2 2023, +6.2% in Q3 2023, +3.8% in Q4 2023, then fell −5.4% in Q1 2024—the first negative quarterly print in the window.

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside drivers**:
- P/E reversion to the midpoint of its 1-year range (~22.3×) on current TTM EPS of ~$23.93 (sum of last 4 quarters) would imply a price of ~$534, representing +7.9% from the current $494.70.
- P/B reversion to the range midpoint (~5.4×) on current book value per share (~$100.51) implies ~$543, a +9.7% move.
- Revenue growth remains robust: +14.1% YoY on a TTM base of $371.6B, with TTM net income of $23.14B.
- Equity has compounded at +15.9% YoY, expanding the book-value floor beneath valuation.

**Downside drivers**:
- Q4 2023 margin compression: operating margin fell to 8.1% from Q3's 9.2%, and net margin to 6.0% from 6.5%, despite revenue growth. If this trend persists, forward earnings growth may disappoint relative to the trailing 16.3% pace.
- The stock has already broken its positive quarterly streak, declining −5.4% in Q1 2024, and sits near the bottom of its quarterly price dynamics.
- No cash-flow data: the inability to verify free cash flow introduces information risk. If operating cash flow materially trails net income ($23.14B TTM), earnings quality concerns would intensify.
- The moderate leverage (liabilities/equity = 1.85x) limits balance-sheet flexibility in a stress scenario, though the consistent equity build provides a buffer.
- P/E at 20.73 is already compressed; further derating to the range floor (~19.8) would imply roughly −4.5% downside on current earnings.

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

UNH presents a **mixed risk/reward profile** based purely on the numbers. The strongest quantitative positive is the combination of robust double-digit revenue and earnings growth (+14.1% and +16.3% YoY, respectively) alongside valuation multiples that have compressed to the lower end of their one-year ranges (P/E 20.73 vs. range 19.8–24.8; P/B 4.91 vs. range 4.7–6.1). A mean reversion to mid-range multiples on current earnings implies ~8–10% upside.

On the downside, the Q4 2023 margin compression (operating margin 8.1% vs. 9.2% Q3) and sequential EPS decline (−6.3% QoQ) are early-warning signals. The −5.4% quarterly price decline in Q1 2024, breaking a positive trend, aligns with deteriorating near-term earnings momentum. The absence of all cash-flow data prevents confirmation of cash-generation quality.

**Net assessment**: The stock is trading at a modestly depressed valuation relative to its own history, but this discount is partly justified by visible margin softness and slowing sequential growth. Without cash-flow confirmation and with margin trajectory uncertain, the risk/reward appears **balanced to slightly favorable** for a fundamental investor with a multi-quarter horizon, contingent on margin stabilization. The key quantitative tension is whether the 14%+ revenue growth can reaccelerate operating margins, or whether the Q4 compression is the beginning of a structural margin headwind.
UNH.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: UnitedHealth Group (UNH)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the supplied narrative, UnitedHealth Group (UNH) operates through two principal segments: UnitedHealthcare (health insurance) and Optum (health services including care delivery, pharmacy benefits, and technology). The company is described as having "robust financial performance with significant market capitalization," with Optum and UnitedHealthcare driving "diversified revenue streams" (2024-02-29). A key facet of its business is a substantial "merger and acquisition strategy, which has bolstered its capabilities and solidified its nationwide presence" (2024-03-24). The company's top line is noted to "continue to gain on the back of" these segments and strategic actions (2024-03-22).

### 2. Recent Developments
*   **Cybersecurity Incident (Impact Ongoing):** On 2024-02-21, UNH's subsidiary, Change Healthcare, experienced a significant cyberattack. As of 2024-03-21, this attack continued to impact healthcare services, leading to an investigation by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to determine any breach of protected health data.
*   **Legal & Regulatory Actions:**
    *   **Cybersecurity-Related Lawsuits:** Multiple class-action lawsuits have been filed (noted 2024-03-13, 2024-03-14, 2024-03-25) related to the data breach. Reports indicate UNH had not yet provided a mandatory federal notification for breaches affecting over 500 people as of mid-March 2024.
    *   **Antitrust Investigation:** The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) is conducting an antitrust probe into UNH's dealings with healthcare providers (noted 2024-02-28, 2024-03-05). One report from 2024-03-05 alleges a corporate culture that pressures employees to limit coverage.
*   **Corporate Strategy & Outlook:**
    *   **Growth Catalysts:** The company is cited as pursuing growth via acquisitions, partnerships, and expansion of its telehealth services (2024-03-22, 2024-03-24).
    *   **2024 Financial Guidance:** As of 2024-03-22, UNH's adjusted net earnings per share were anticipated to be between $27.50 and $28.00 for 2024, suggesting approximately 10.5% growth from 2023.
    *   **Stock Performance:** On 2024-03-26, UNH's stock closed at $492.31, noted as outperforming a falling market on that day.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risks:**
    1.  **Operational & Financial Disruption from Cyberattack:** The breach at Change Healthcare, which processes a substantial volume of U.S. medical claims, presents ongoing operational, reputational, and financial risks from service disruption, remediation costs, and regulatory penalties.
    2.  **Heightened Legal Liability:** Multiple active class-action lawsuits related to the data breach create potential financial liability and reputational damage.
    3.  **Regulatory & Antitrust Overhang:** The active HHS investigation into the breach and the DOJ antitrust probe into its provider relationships introduce material uncertainty and could constrain future strategic moves, including acquisitions.
    4.  **Allegations of Corporate Practices:** Reports of internal pressure to limit coverage could invite further scrutiny and damage public/ regulatory perception (2024-03-05).
*   **Offsets:**
    1.  **Diversified Business Model:** The combination of insurance (UnitedHealthcare) and health services (Optum) provides structural resilience and multiple growth avenues.
    2.  **Proven Strategic Execution:** A history of growth through mergers and acquisitions is cited as a core strength (2024-03-24).
    3.  **Market Position & Demand:** The company operates in a large, essential market with its telehealth suite noted as being "in-demand" (2024-03-22).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral/Cautious.** The fundamental franchise remains large, diversified, and is guiding for continued earnings growth in 2024. However, the near-term narrative is dominated by significant and material operational and legal headwinds stemming from the February 2024 cyberattack, coupled with an active DOJ antitrust investigation. These risks create substantial uncertainty regarding potential financial impacts, regulatory outcomes, and management distraction, offsetting the underlying business strength as of the evidence date.

*Evidence Separation: The business description, 2024 guidance, and growth strategies are cited from specific dated articles. The risks related to the cyberattack's impact, lawsuits, and investigations are directly cited from dated reports. No financial multiples or figures beyond those in the text have been used.*

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Net margin: Stable at 6.2% throughout most of the supplied period, with a slight tick to 6.2% and ROE at 24.5% at the latest point.' This is internally confusing — it says net margin is 'stable at 6.2%' with 'a slight tick to 6.2%', which is a non-statement. The raw valuation data shows net_margin=0.062 at the latest point, consistent with the income statement (5.67B/94.43B = 6.0% for Q4). The report also earlier correctly states Q4 net margin as 6.0%, creating a minor internal inconsistency with the 6.2% figure in section 3. The 6.2% figure comes from the valuation ratios panel (which may use a different trailing period), but this discrepancy is not explained.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the 6.2% net margin in the valuation panel likely reflects a trailing-12-month calculation, while the Q4 quarterly net margin is 6.0%. Note the distinction to avoid reader confusion.

corrections.issue: The quant report's section 2 states 'ROE (TTM) is reported at 24.5%, modestly below its recent range peak of 25.3% (April 2023).' The raw valuation data shows roe=0.253 at 2023-04-03 and roe=0.245 at 2024-03-28, so the 25.3% figure is supported. However, the range in the raw data is listed as 'roe 0.2..0.3', which is a very wide bucket. The report's characterization of 24.5% as 'modestly below' 25.3% is accurate but the claim that this is a 'range peak' is only supported by the 12-point sparse history, not a continuous range. This is acceptable but worth noting as a minor precision issue.
corrections.required_action: No action required — the claim is supported by the sparse history data points provided.

corrections.issue: The qual report references '2024-03-05' allegations of corporate culture pressuring employees to limit coverage. The raw qualitative evidence confirms this via the NationofChange article dated 2024-03-05. However, the qual report also states 'One report from 2024-03-05 alleges a corporate culture that pressures employees to limit coverage' in the Legal & Regulatory section, which is supported. The qual report does not mention the $10M BlackCat ransomware reward from the 2024-03-28 Wall Street Breakfast podcast, which is tangentially relevant to the Change Healthcare cyberattack context. This is a minor omission but could inform the severity assessment of the cyber risk.
corrections.required_action: Consider noting the State Department's $10M reward for BlackCat ransomware group intelligence (2024-03-28) as additional context for the Change Healthcare cyberattack severity, as it signals high-level government attention to the threat actor involved.

The 6.2% figure comes from the valuation ratios panel but the report does not reconcile the two figures.

: The quant report states 'The balance sheet expanded by $52.5B in total assets over this period (from $221.24B to $273.72B)'. Raw data confirms: 2022-03-31 total_assets=221.24B and 2023-12-31 total_assets=273.72B, difference=$52.48B. This is supported.
